raw_consumer_statement:
My XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX credit card with Barclays was locked due to fraudulent transactions several months ago. I had my balance paid down to zero, and didn't like the card, so I just stopped using it instead of unlocking it. Because I was not using it, and had been notified that it had been locked, I did not log in to check my balance, as there was no reason for it to be anything other than zero. However, I started getting messages to make payments, and when I logged in I found that several fraudulent transactions had occurred from a merchant called " gmrorg.com '' that I do not recognize. I called the Barclays customer service line and spoke to a representative, to whom I explained the situation. I asked that all transactions from gmrorg.com be reported as fraudulent, and that my account be closed immediately. I made it clear that I did not expect to pay those balances, because they were fraudulent charges. I asked for written confirmation that the transactions were reported as fraudulent and that my account was to be closed. The representative refused to provide any written confirmation. 

A few weeks later, I saw that no such thing had happened, so I called back again. The second representative I spoke to said that the transactions were never reported as fraudulent. I was upset and asked them to make sure that they were reported as fraudulent. They transferred me to another department and I went through a long process of confirming the transactions I wanted to report as fraudulent. Once again, I asked for written confirmation that the account was being closed and that the transactions were reported as fraudulent and refunded. The representatives refused to provide written confirmation again, but told me verbally that they had done so. 

Now, months later, I noticed that my credit score dropped, so I checked my account and see that I have been charged multiple late payment fees and that the transactions still have not been reported as fraudulent. At this point it is senseless to continue trying to rectify this with Barclays directly as they have openly lied to me multiple times and have done none of the things they said they would after multiple attempts by me to contact and address these issues.

I have now paid the account balance because I don't want it to further affect my credit score, but this in no way indicates that I accept responsibility for these amounts. I expect that all fees and fraudulent transactions be refunded to my checking account.
